intro: AppMetrica exposes a free tier while AppsFlyer starts at $0.05-0.07/install (Growth). AppsFlyer is positioned for mobile, while AppMetrica leans toward indie or cost-sensitive teams in cis markets wanting free analytics plus attribution. In practice, AppsFlyer — Mobile attribution and marketing analytics platform with deterministic matching, deep links, and Privacy Sandbox support. Yandexs free real-time mobile app analytics and install attribution platform with crash reporting and push notifications. AppsFlyer scores 5/5 on developer experience versus 4/5 for AppMetrica. AppMetrica rates 5/5 on transparency versus 3/5 for AppsFlyer. Listed strengths unique to AppsFlyer include mobile apps with paid acquisition, while AppMetrica lists free with unlimited events and real-time reports on its side. Reach for AppsFlyer when you need mobile apps with paid acquisition; reach for AppMetrica when the job is free mobile attribution and analytics for apps with yandex ad traffic. The honest trade-off: AppsFlyer is not built for web-only products, and AppMetrica is not the right call for eu or us apps with strict vendor, sanctions or data-residency policies, so neither is a universal default.
quick_take: AppsFlyer is for mobile; AppMetrica is for indie or cost-sensitive teams in cis markets wanting free analytics plus attribution; decide based on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose AppsFlyer if mobile and the $0.05-0.07/install (Growth) entry price fits your budget.
verdict_b: Choose AppMetrica if indie or cost-sensitive teams in cis markets wanting free analytics plus attribution and you need a free tier to validate fit.

faq.a: AppMetrica has a free tier while AppsFlyer starts at $0.05-0.07/install (Growth), so at the lowest end AppMetrica costs less. Above the free limits, per-unit rates decide the winner, and those aren't in the summary data here. Run the numbers on your expected volume.

faq.q: Can I migrate from AppsFlyer to AppMetrica?
faq.a: Technically you can move between any two tools in the same category, but effort varies: leaving AppsFlyer is moderate effort at medium lock-in (Medium — attribution data tied to to AppsFlyer ecosystem), and onboarding to AppMetrica involves medium lock-in (Yandex analytics, data export limited). Plan for data export, re-integration, and a parallel-run window rather than a hard cutover.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, AppsFlyer or AppMetrica?
faq.a: On the 0-5 developer experience score in our data, AppsFlyer rates 5/5 while AppMetrica rates 4/5, giving AppsFlyer the edge. Scores compress a lot of variance though — SDK language coverage, error messages, and docs quality matter more than the number. Run a quick integration spike on both before trusting the gap.

faq.q: Is AppMetrica a good alternative to AppsFlyer?
faq.a: AppMetrica is an alternative in the same category, but its stated sweet spot is indie or cost-sensitive teams in cis markets wanting free analytics plus attribution, while AppsFlyer's is mobile. If your job matches AppsFlyer's profile more closely, switching to AppMetrica is a compromise. Use the best-fit match, not brand familiarity, to decide. Note: AppMetrica is explicitly not for eu or us apps with strict vendor, sanctions or data-residency policies.
